title: /context init <dir> silently ignores dir argument when .pi-context.json exists; no /context switch family for bootstrap-pointer flip + bidirectional substrate-dir navigation; blocks DEC-0036 cutover + denies operators the substrate-management primitive parallel to git switch
description: initProject at packages/pi-context/src/index.ts:266 writes the bootstrap pointer ONLY when .pi-context.json does NOT exist (condition: `if (!fs.existsSync(path.join(cwd, '.pi-context.json'))) writeBootstrapPointer(cwd, contextDir);`). When an existing pointer is present, the caller-supplied contextDir argument is completely discarded. The function proceeds with resolveContextDir(cwd) which reads the EXISTING pointer (line 267) and operates against the existing substrate dir, not the user-supplied one. Result message reports skipped against the existing dir while the user's argument named a different dir. 'Project initialized' message is misleading — project was already initialized for existing dir; user's explicit new-dir request was silently dropped. DEC-0036 (enacted 2026-05-17) declares the canonical re-derivation path with consequence #7 'Cutover (bootstrap-pointer flip + .project archive)' as the closing step. The framework has no canonical surface for that flip OR for the broader substrate-management primitive that bidirectional switching would enable. Operators need the substrate-management equivalent of git switch — flip pointer to existing substrate dir; bootstrap new substrate dir and flip; flip back to previous; list discoverable substrates; archive deprecated substrates. The substrate-as-bounded-context property (each substrate internally coherent; cross-substrate references explicit + sentinel-typed) is preserved under multi-substrate navigation. Bidirectional capability unlocks workflow shapes that do not exist today: transitional A/B during cutover, experimental substrate trees parallel to canonical, per-feature substrate scratch spaces, read-only consultation of frozen substrates without ceremony.

evidence.reference: initProject body: `if (!fs.existsSync(path.join(cwd, '.pi-context.json'))) writeBootstrapPointer(cwd, contextDir);` — pointer write is conditional on absence; with existing pointer, caller's contextDir argument is ignored

Function body after the conditional pointer-write proceeds with resolveContextDir(cwd) which reads existing pointer; dir creation operates against resolved path not against the user-supplied argument; returns created/skipped lists against the wrong dir

Enacted 2026-05-17, consequence #7 verbatim: 'Cutover (FGAP-026 Phase 10) is a bootstrap-pointer flip + .project archive, not a data migration.' The cutover step depends on a canonical surface for the pointer flip that does not exist.
Current state: {"contextDir": ".project", "version": "1.0.0", "created_at": "2026-05-10T05:27:24.196Z"} — pointer dates from 2026-05-10; pre-dates DEC-0036; no surface to flip it canonically

Operator-experience target: 'I never guess. I see the agent's health at a glance.' Same discipline applies to substrate operations: an init command that silently ignores its argument and reports success under a no-op violates the same operator-experience contract — the operator can't tell the framework did the wrong thing

Re-survey of /Users/david/Projects/clock-menu-app/.context shows init works correctly there because clock-menu-app's .pi-context.json was created at the same time as .context/ (no pre-existing pointer); the bug surfaces only when an existing pointer is being replaced — the exact DEC-0036 cutover scenario

impact: Blocks DEC-0036-mandated .context/ cutover for every existing pi-context user-substrate. Operators following the DEC-0036-directed path get silent no-op + misleading success message. Workaround paths violate canonical-authoring discipline: direct .pi-context.json edit bypasses pi-dispatch auth-gate (FGAP-134/138) + dispatch attestation; manual .context/ scaffolding bypasses install-ceremony invariants. Beyond the immediate cutover blocker: absence of a /context switch family denies operators the substrate-management primitive needed for transitional cutover work (substrates side-by-side during transition), experimental substrate design (try new block-kind shapes in throwaway substrate without affecting canonical), per-arc substrate scratch (focused work in isolated substrate then merge back), and read-only consultation of frozen substrates (DEC-0036's archaeological .project/ access pattern). The operator-experience parity with git's substrate-of-record management (git switch + git branch) is the model; substrate-management today lacks even the basic flip primitive.
canonical_vocabulary: bootstrap-pointer flip; substrate dir switch; cutover canonical surface
proposed_resolution:
/context switch command family modeled on git switch, with bootstrap-pointer flip as one mode among bidirectional substrate-dir navigation operations. Command surface: (a) `/context switch <existing-dir>` — flip pointer to existing substrate dir; validate target is a valid substrate (has config.json) before flipping; (b) `/context switch -c <new-dir>` — bootstrap new substrate dir (writeBootstrapPointer + create dirs via initProject-equivalent) AND flip pointer to it; (c) `/context switch -` — flip to previous contextDir per pointer.previous_contextDir field; (d) `/context list` companion — list discoverable substrate dirs in cwd (scan for dirs with config.json); (e) `/context archive <dir>` separate command — move substrate dir to archive/<dir>/ + write-protect; non-default operation requiring explicit invocation (substrate-delete more dangerous than git branch -d so no `-d` flag on switch). Pointer-shape extension v1.0.0 → v1.1.0: add optional `previous_contextDir`, `switched_at`, `switched_by` fields. Migrated via existing write-schema-migration mechanism per FGAP-136 (identity migration; old pointer shape remains readable). Cross-substrate semantics: edges within relations.json are substrate-scoped; cross-substrate references use sentinel form (`<substrate-name>:<id>` or `project-archive:<id>`); new relation_type `references_cross_substrate_item` carries explicit cross-substrate edges; lazy resolution via consultation tool. Auth-gate routing: every switch operation modifies the pointer = structural substrate change with significant operator-flow consequence; routes through pi-dispatch auth-gate (FGAP-134/138) for writer.kind=human attestation + interactive confirmation; switch history auditable via pointer's switched_at + switched_by fields (potentially extended to switch-history array). initProject argument-handling fix: when existing pointer differs from caller's contextDir, fail loudly with structured guide-message pointing to `/context switch -c <dir>` as the correct command rather than silently dropping the argument. The init + switch commands form a guided-handoff: init bootstraps fresh substrate when none exists; switch handles all subsequent substrate-dir transitions. DEC-0036's cutover step becomes the first use of `/context switch -c .context` (operator-directed, auth-gate-confirmed, attestation-stamped).
